title: Common Prompting Pitfalls
concept: Vague prompts produce generic results; overly long or contradictory prompts can confuse the model, producing muddled or unpredictable outputs.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI: 'Write something good.' What will you probably get?

options.text: A random, generic result that may not match what you wanted
Exactly what you imagined, every time
An error message because the AI can't respond

exercises.explain: Vague prompts like 'write something good' don't tell the AI what topic, style, or purpose you want, so it just guesses — often landing on something generic.

cards.title: Why Did I Get THAT?!
cards.body: Ever typed a quick prompt and gotten a weird, boring, or totally off-target answer? It's probably not the AI's fault — it's a prompting pitfall! Let's learn the traps to avoid so your prompts actually get you what you want.

cards.title: What Are Prompting Pitfalls?
cards.body: Prompting pitfalls are common mistakes that lead to bad AI results. Vague prompts (too little info) give generic, boring answers. Prompts that are too long or contain contradictions (mixed-up instructions) confuse the model and produce muddled, unpredictable outputs.

cards.title: Ordering Pizza Badly
cards.body: Saying 'give me food' gets you a random meal — that's a vague prompt. Saying 'give me a large spicy vegetarian pizza but also make it not spicy and put meat on it' confuses the cook — that's a contradictory prompt. Clear, specific orders get the best pizza (and the best AI answers)!

cards.title: Pitfalls in Action
cards.body:
Vague: 'Tell me about animals.' → You get a bland, generic overview.
Contradictory: 'Write a short essay that is at least 10 pages long and only uses one sentence.' → The AI gets confused because the instructions clash.
Better: 'Write a 3-sentence fun fact about octopuses for a 10-year-old.' → Clear, specific, and useful!

cards.title: Myth: More Words = Better Prompt
cards.body: Myth: Adding tons of extra details always makes a prompt better. Truth: Piling on unrelated or conflicting instructions can overwhelm the AI, causing it to mix things up or ignore parts of your request. Clear and focused beats long and cluttered.

cards.title: Vague vs. Clear Prompt
cards.body: Compare how a vague prompt and a clear, focused prompt lead to very different quality results.

left.text: 'Write about space.' → Generic, unfocused paragraph that could be about anything.

right.text: 'Write 3 fun facts about Saturn's rings for a kid.' → Specific, useful, on-topic answer.

exercises.prompt: Which prompt is most likely to produce a generic, unhelpful answer?

options.text: 'Write a 2-sentence description of a red bicycle for a toy ad.'
'List 3 benefits of drinking water, in bullet points.'
'Explain photosynthesis in one simple sentence for a 9-year-old.'

exercises.explain: 'Tell me something interesting' gives no topic, length, or audience — so the AI has to guess, usually landing on something generic.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Giving an AI two contradictory instructions in the same prompt (like 'make it short but write 10 paragraphs') can confuse the model and lead to muddled output.
exercises.explain: True! Contradictory instructions confuse the AI because it can't satisfy both requests at once, often resulting in a messy or unpredictable answer.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the idea about prompting.
exercises.sentence: A prompt that is too ______ or full of contradictions can confuse the AI and produce a muddled response.
exercises.explain: Overly long or contradictory prompts can overwhelm the model with too much or conflicting information, leading to confused, muddled output.

exercises.prompt: Spot the AI: Which response below sounds like it came from an AI confused by a vague prompt ('Tell me about stuff')?

options.text: 'Stuff' can refer to many things — objects, ideas, or general matter. Here is some general information: things exist, people use them, and they can be useful or not useful depending on the situation.
The Great Wall of China is over 13,000 miles long and was built over centuries to protect ancient Chinese states from invasions.
Photosynthesis is the process plants use to turn sunlight into energy, using carbon dioxide and water.
A hexagon is a shape with six sides and six angles.

exercises.explain: The first response is vague and generic, filled with filler about 'stuff' — a clear sign the original prompt didn't give the AI enough specific direction.

exercises.prompt: You want an AI to help you write a birthday card message. Which prompt avoids BOTH major pitfalls (vagueness and contradiction)?

options.text: 'Write a very long two-word birthday message that is formal but also silly and also serious.'
'Write a short, funny 2-sentence birthday message for my 10-year-old cousin who loves dinosaurs.'

exercises.explain: Option C is specific (topic, length, tone, audience) without piling on conflicting instructions — it avoids both being too vague and being contradictory, so the AI can give a clear, useful answer.
